nodes.text: Stranger, I am Elder Aldric of Ironveil. The Veil — the ancient barrier that has protected us for centuries — is fracturing. A goblin warlord named Gorak the Ironbreaker has seized the Veil Stone in the northern castle. Without it, Ironveil will fall. Will you face Gorak?

choices.text: I will stop Gorak and save the Veil.

nodes.text: The village is counting on you. Gorak holds the northern castle. Seek allies in the village before you go — you will need them. Return victorious.

Name's Roan. Slimes have flooded the eastern cave — they are poisoning the water supply. I have been tracking them for days but there are too many for one hunter. Clear out three of them and the cave will be safe again.
Defeat three slimes. They spit acid and travel in packs — do not let them surround you. I will be here when you return.

I am Kael Thornback. Ironveil has two answers to the goblin threat. The Iron Guard will march on Gorak head-on — steel against steel. The Veil Mages believe the barrier can be mended from within, ending the threat without a war. Both factions need a capable warrior. Where does your allegiance lie?

choices.text: With the Iron Guard — we fight fire with steel.
With the Veil Mages — the Veil must be restored.

nodes.text: A warrior's choice. Report to Captain Vera at the garrison — she has your first orders. Fight well.
A thoughtful choice. Seek Arch-Mage Solen in the tower to the east. She holds the key to mending the Veil.
Captain Vera, Iron Guard. Goblin scouts have been probing the village perimeter — Gorak is mapping our defenses before the main assault. Take them down before they report back.

choices.text: I am not with the Guard.

nodes.text: Good. Eliminate two goblin scouts. They move fast and communicate by whistle — kill them before they signal. Dismissed.
Then you have no business at this garrison. Join the Iron Guard if you want a part in this fight.

I am Arch-Mage Solen. The Veil is anchored by three Veil Stones — Gorak holds one. I have located a second, but the path is dangerous. Take this Veil Shard: it will resonate near any Veil Stone and confirm its location. Will you retrieve it?

choices.text: I will find the Veil Stone.
I fight for the Guard, not the Mages.

nodes.text: The Shard will guide you. Bring back what you find — every Stone we recover weakens Gorak's hold on the Veil.
Then may the Guard's steel hold where the Veil cannot. I hope you are right about the direct approach.
Sergeant Davan, Iron Guard. Captain Vera sent you? Good. The garrison at north gate is stretched thin. We need your blade on the march.
Fall in. But watch yourself — Officer Crest has been acting strange lately. Keep your ears open.
Then you have no place in this formation. Move along.
Officer Crest. I... did not expect visitors. Whatever you heard — it is not what it looks like.

choices.text: I am reporting this to the Captain.

nodes.text: You will regret this. The Guard is not as clean as you think.
Wise. Some things are better left unseen. I will remember this.

Scholar Lira. I have been deciphering the ruin texts near the eastern cave — the Veil was not always a barrier. Once it was a living lattice, and I think I know how to reactivate it. Arch-Mage Solen must hear this.
Bring this to Solen. But warn him — the reactivation will draw deeply from the caster. He needs to know the cost before he commits.
Then I hope the Guard's steel is enough. Ancient magic is not so easily ignored.
I have read Lira's findings. The lattice reactivation is possible — but it will cost me everything. I go into this knowing that. Unless... did you come to warn me?

choices.text: I am warning you. Find another way.
The Veil must be mended. Do what you must.

nodes.text: A warning, then. I will not waste it. Perhaps there is a way to split the cost across more anchors. I will try.
Then it is decided. Whatever comes — the Veil will hold. That is what matters.
Gorak's Lieutenant guards the Veil Ruins. He knows you are coming. Both factions must move now — together or not at all.
Then we march. The ruins are ahead. Stay sharp.
When you are ready. He will not wait forever.
The sanctum opens before you. Beyond lies Gorak's throne — the final confrontation awaits. Are you prepared?

choices.text: I am ready. Let us end this.

nodes.text: Then the Veil itself watches your final step. Victory or death — this is the end.
The sanctum will wait. Return when you are ready to face your fate.
The war is over. The Veil is restored. What comes next?

choices.text: I will stand with the Guard.

nodes.text: The Iron Guard welcomes you, hero. The kingdom is safer with you at our side.
The Veil Mages honor your sacrifice. The lattice glows brighter with your name in its runes.
A wanderer's heart, yet tied to Ironveil's fate. The village will remember you always.
